myth: TCP is always slower than UDP
truth: TCP adds ~1 RTT for handshake and has congestion control overhead, but modern TCP (BBR congestion control, TFO) is very fast. QUIC (HTTP/3) runs over UDP but reimplements TCP-like reliability — showing that raw UDP isn't automatically better.

myth: The OSI model is how the internet actually works
truth: The internet uses the TCP/IP 4-layer model. OSI is a teaching framework. Real protocols don't map cleanly to 7 layers — TLS spans session/presentation/transport, HTTP spans application/presentation.

myth: A switch and router do the same thing
truth: Switches operate at Layer 2 (MAC addresses, within a LAN). Routers operate at Layer 3 (IP addresses, between networks). Modern L3 switches blur the line but the conceptual distinction matters.
